questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Carousal
questions.explanation: Carousal – a drunken party or celebrationMourn – to feel or show great sadnessAppreciate – admireRejoice - to feel or show great happiness.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the highlight part of the sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement'. In no case you are not permitted to cross the boundary of the hostel.
questions.explanation: The sentence should not be double negative. “In any case” is the correct option. C cannot be correct because with some it should be cases not case and with any case is the correct use.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Shyam tried to tell us/ what has happened/ but he was not audible.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in past tense. So, “what had happened” is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. crestfallen
questions.explanation: Crestfallen – dejected, disappointedEbullient – full of confidence and energy.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Defer
questions.explanation: Defer - postponeDisprove – to show something as falseAffirm – to support something firmly

questions.question: Select the option that will substitute the highlight part of the given sentence. In case no substitution is needed, select ‘No substitution required’. It was me who had to face the consequences.
questions.explanation: “It was I” is the correct use as it is the nominative case.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. At a pinch
questions.explanation: At a pinch – in a bad situation when help is needed.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Reckoned to be the pride of Kashmir tourism, Dal Lake is the most popular attraction in Srinagar. B. Enclosed by splendid Pir Panjal mountains and Mughal Gardens, this must-see place is ideal for family vacationers, honeymoon couples, and even for solo travellers seeking peace. C. Dal lake is also one of the best locations in Kashmir for film shooting as it offers some incredible vistas of lofty rolling hills and distant snow-capped mountains. D. The houseboats on the lake are also the top attraction as the best accommodation option in Srinagar to the tourists.
questions.explanation: A is the introductory statement so, it must be the starting statement. Hence, c is the correct option.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. It ………………… raining for two hours.
questions.explanation: Present perfect continuous. Sub + has/have + been + V1 + ing + since/for + time

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Perseverance is the correct spelling.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Imran has found two good jobs in the advertisements of the newspaper.
options: Two good jobs has found by Imran in the advertisements of the newspaper.
Two good jobs have been found by Imran in the advertisements of the newspaper.
Two good jobs are being found by Imran in the advertisements of the newspaper.
Two good jobs had found by Imran in the advertisements of the newspaper.
questions.explanation: The given sentence is in present perfect tense. While changing speech the rule says; Active ---> Sub + has/have +v3 + obj Passive --> Obj + has/have + been + v3 + by + sub

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Scorpion

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. Lalitha should avail of / this opportunity / to meet her father / this summer.
questions.explanation: “avail” should be followed by the reflexive pronoun “herself”.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. But a dove that happened to be sitting on a neighbouring tree saw the ant’s danger and, plucking off a leaf, let it drop into the water before him. B. Just at that time, a fowler was spreading his net and was in the act of ensnaring the dove, when the ant, perceiving his object, bit his heel. C. An ant went to a fountain to quench his thirst and, tumbling in, almost drowned. D. The ant mounting upon it, was presently wafted safely ashore.
questions.explanation: Considering the sequence of events, a is the correct option

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. exterminate
questions.explanation: Exterminate – eliminate, abolish

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Making something impure by adding something undesirable
questions.explanation: Debilitate – to make someone weakPetrify – change into a stony substanceAugmentation – the action of making something bigger in size.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. Sneha said to me, “I was playing cricket.”
options: Sneha said to me that I was playing cricket.
Sneha told me that she was playing cricket.
Sneha told me that I was playing cricket.
Sneha told me that she had been playing cricket.
questions.explanation: Rule; Direct Form Past continuous changes into Past Perfect Continuous in Indirect Form.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who is hard to please
questions.explanation: Diffident – one who does not express himself much.Bashful – someone who is shyEffigy – dummy

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Get into a soup
questions.explanation: get into a soup – to get into a bad situation

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Sachin Tendulkar and Ricky Ponting were cricketers of the same period; they were ______.
questions.explanation: Acquaintances – a person that you know but who is not your close friendColleagues – co workersAssociates – a person you get to know because of workContemporaries – a person who lives or does something at the same time as somebody elseD is the most appropriate option

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The Panchatantra is a legendary collection of short stories from India. (1)______ composed in the 2nd century BC, Panchatantra is (2)______ to be written by Vishnu Sharma (3)______ with many other scholars. The purpose behind the composition (4)______ to implant moral values and governing (5)______ in the young sons of the king. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: Originally is the most appropriate word to fit in the given blank.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The Panchatantra is a legendary collection of short stories from India. (1)______ composed in the 2nd century BC, Panchatantra is (2)______ to be written by Vishnu Sharma (3)______ with many other scholars. The purpose behind the composition (4)______ to implant moral values and governing (5)______ in the young sons of the king. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: believe – accept something to be true without proof.Alleged – said without proofDesigned – conceived for a specific purposeEstablished – something that is existing for a long time and therefore is well recognized.Believed is the most appropriate word.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The Panchatantra is a legendary collection of short stories from India. (1)______ composed in the 2nd century BC, Panchatantra is (2)______ to be written by Vishnu Sharma (3)______ with many other scholars. The purpose behind the composition (4)______ to implant moral values and governing (5)______ in the young sons of the king. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: along with – in addition to

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The Panchatantra is a legendary collection of short stories from India. (1)______ composed in the 2nd century BC, Panchatantra is (2)______ to be written by Vishnu Sharma (3)______ with many other scholars. The purpose behind the composition (4)______ to implant moral values and governing (5)______ in the young sons of the king. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: was is the correct helping verb as the sentence is in the past tense.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. The Panchatantra is a legendary collection of short stories from India. (1)______ composed in the 2nd century BC, Panchatantra is (2)______ to be written by Vishnu Sharma (3)______ with many other scholars. The purpose behind the composition (4)______ to implant moral values and governing (5)______ in the young sons of the king. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: skills is the most appropriate word out of the given.
